evals.prompt: Create a 3-slide presentation deck explaining what WebComponents are. Import html-deck version 1.0.0-alpha.2 directly from unpkg (https://unpkg.com/html-deck@1.0.0-alpha.2) in a module script tag. Slide 1: Cover layout. Slide 2: Standard layout with a text list and a callout highlighting benefits. Slide 3: Split layout with standard components. Style slide 2's callout using standard theme colors. Do not write any custom styles, style attributes, or css files.
evals.expected_output: A clean HTML slideshow loading html-deck@1.0.0-alpha.2 from unpkg containing standard layout elements and no custom styles/attributes.

evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML presentation slide that loads html-deck@1.0.0-alpha.2 directly from unpkg. In an inline <style> block, design a custom dark neon theme under the :root selector. The background must be pitch black (#050505), body text bright green (#10b981), primary accent hot pink (#ff007f), and secondary accent neon blue (#00f2fe). Do not use element or class selectors. Derive secondary colors using relative color syntax.
evals.expected_output: A standalone HTML slide deck file index.html with unpkg loader and inline <style> dark neon variables under :root.

evals.prompt: Create a standalone HTML slide deck that loads html-deck@1.0.0-alpha.2 from unpkg. In an inline <style> block, define a custom corporate presentation theme under the :root selector. It must include header/footer offset configurations, and apply a compact sizing scale (fonts and gaps) and proper heading top padding safety calculations to avoid overlaps, specifically optimized for dense academic slides. Design it with a professional slate-blue color palette. Ensure high readability (WCAG AA contrast >= 4.5:1) and derive secondary colors using relative color syntax.
evals.expected_output: A standalone HTML slide deck index.html. The inline <style> must use small fonts and gaps suited for high density, establish correct padding relations relative to the header offset/font-size to avoid overlap, satisfy WCAG AA contrast, and derive secondary colors from base colors.
